L. 111–87section 703 of Pub. L. 109–415Pub. L. 111–87Pub. L. 111–87section 300ff–11 of this titleSept. 30, 2009Sept. 30, 2009Sept. 30, 2009

Effective Date of 1996 AmendmentAmendment by sections 3(c)(5), (g)(2) and 6(c)(3)(A) of effective , and amendment by sections 5 and 6(c)(3)(B) of effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 104–146Pub. L. 104–146section 13 of Pub. L. 104–146section 300ff–11 of this titleOct. 1, 1996May 20, 1996

children.heading: Amount of grant to State
children.label: a Amount of grant to State
I
children.text: with respect to a covered State that has less than 90 living cases of AIDS, as determined under paragraph (2)(D), $200,000; or

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to a covered State that has 90 or more living cases of AIDS, as determined under paragraph (2)(D), $500,000; and

children.label: ii
children.text: an amount determined under paragraph (2) and then, as applicable, increased under paragraph (2)(H); and

children.label: B
children.text: each territory other than Guam and the Virgin Islands shall be the greater of $50,000 or an amount determined under paragraph (2).

children.label: i
children.text: an amount equal to the amount made available under for the fiscal year involved for grants pursuant to paragraph (1), subject to subparagraph (F); andsection 300ff–31b of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the product of 0.75 and the ratio of the State distribution factor for the State or territory (as determined under subsection (B)) to the sum of the respective State distribution factors for all States or territories;

children.label: II
children.text: the product of .20 and the ratio of the non-EMA distribution factor for the State or territory (as determined under subparagraph (C)) to the sum of the respective non-EMA distribution factors for all States or territories; and

children.label: III
children.text: if the State does not for such fiscal year contain any area that is an eligible area under subpart I of part A or any area that is a transitional area under (referred to in this subclause as a “no-EMA State”), the product of 0.05 and the ratio of the number of cases that applies for the State under subparagraph (D) to the sum of the respective numbers of cases that so apply for all no-EMA States.section 300ff–19 of this title

children.heading: State distribution factor
children.label: B State distribution factor
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A)(ii)(I), the term “State distribution factor” means an amount equal to the number of living cases of HIV/AIDS in the State involved, as determined under subparagraph (D).

children.label: i
children.text: the number of living cases of HIV/AIDS in the State involved, as determined under subparagraph (D); less

children.label: I
children.text: the total number of living cases of HIV/AIDS that are within areas in such State that are eligible areas under subpart I of part A for the fiscal year involved, which individual number for an area is the number that applies under for the area for such fiscal year; andsection 300ff–11 of this title

children.label: II
children.text: the total number of such cases that are within areas in such State that are transitional areas under for such fiscal year, which individual number for an area is the number that applies under such section for the fiscal year.section 300ff–19 of this title

children.heading: Requirement of names-based reporting
children.label: i Requirement of names-based reporting
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), the number determined under this subparagraph for a State for a fiscal year for purposes of subparagraph (B) is the number of living names-based cases of HIV/AIDS in the State that, as of December 31 of the most recent calendar year for which such data is available, have been reported to and confirmed by the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

children.heading: Transition period; exemption regarding non-AIDS cases
children.label: ii Transition period; exemption regarding non-AIDS cases
I
children.text: a system was in operation as of , that provides sufficiently accurate and reliable names-based reporting of such cases throughout the State, subject to clause (vii); orDecember 31, 2005

children.label: II
children.text: no later than the beginning of fiscal year 2008 or a subsequent fiscal year through fiscal year 2012, the Secretary, after consultation with the chief executive of the State, determines that a system has become operational in the State that provides sufficiently accurate and reliable names-based reporting of such cases throughout the State.

children.heading: Requirements for exemption for fiscal year 2007
children.label: iii Requirements for exemption for fiscal year 2007
aa
children.text: the State had submitted to the Secretary a plan for making the transition to sufficiently accurate and reliable names-based reporting of living non-AIDS cases of HIV; or

children.label: bb
children.text: all statutory changes necessary to provide for sufficiently accurate and reliable reporting of such cases had been made; and

children.label: II
children.text: the State had agreed that, by , the State will begin accurate and reliable names-based reporting of such cases, except that such agreement is not required to provide that, as of such date, the system for such reporting be fully sufficient with respect to accuracy and reliability throughout the area.April 1, 2008

children.heading: Requirement for exemption as of fiscal year 2008
children.label: iv Requirement for exemption as of fiscal year 2008
children.text: For each of the fiscal years 2008 through 2012, an exemption under clause (ii) for a State applies only if, as of , the State is substantially in compliance with the agreement under clause (iii)(II).April 1, 2008

children.heading: Progress toward names-based reporting
children.label: v Progress toward names-based reporting
children.text: For fiscal year 2009 or a subsequent fiscal year, the Secretary may terminate an exemption under clause (ii) for a State if the State submitted a plan under clause (iii)(I)(aa) and the Secretary determines that the State is not substantially following the plan.

children.heading: Counting of cases in areas with exemptions
children.label: vi Counting of cases in areas with exemptions

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: With respect to a State that is under a reporting system for living non-AIDS cases of HIV that is not names-based (referred to in this subparagraph as “code-based reporting”), the Secretary shall, for purposes of this subparagraph, modify the number of such cases reported for the State in order to adjust for duplicative reporting in and among systems that use code-based reporting.

children.heading: Adjustment rate
children.label: II Adjustment rate
children.text: The adjustment rate under subclause (I) for a State shall be a reduction of 5 percent for fiscal years before fiscal year 2012 (and 6 percent for fiscal year 2012) in the number of living non-AIDS cases of HIV reported for the State.

children.heading: Increased adjustment for certain States previously using code-based reporting
children.label: III Increased adjustment for certain States previously using code-based reporting
aa
children.text: there is an area in such State that satisfies all of the conditions described in items (aa) through (cc) of ; orsection 300ff–13(a)(3)(C)(vi)(III) of this title

children.label: AA
children.text: fiscal year 2007 was the first year in which the count of living non-AIDS cases of HIV in such area, for purposes of this part, was based on a names-based reporting system; and

children.label: BB
children.text: the amount of funding that such State received under this part for fiscal year 2007 was less than 70 percent of the amount of funding that such State received under such part for fiscal year 2006.

children.heading: List of States meeting standard regardingDecember 31, 2005
children.label: vii List of States meeting standard regardingDecember 31, 2005

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: If a State is specified in subclause (II), the State shall be considered to meet the standard described in clause (ii)(I). No other State may be considered to meet such standard.

children.heading: Relevant States
children.label: II Relevant States
children.text: For purposes of subclause (I), the States specified in this subclause are the following: Alaska, Alabama, Arkansas, Arizona, Colorado, Florida, Indiana, Iowa, Idaho, Kansas, Louisiana, Michigan, Minnesota, Missouri, Mississippi, North Carolina, North Dakota, Nebraska, New Jersey, New Mexico, New York, Nevada, Ohio, Oklahoma, South Carolina, South Dakota, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, Virginia, Wisconsin, West Virginia, Wyoming, Guam, and the Virgin Islands.

children.heading: Rules of construction regarding acceptance of reports
children.label: viii Rules of construction regarding acceptance of reports

children.heading: Cases of AIDS
children.label: I Cases of AIDS
children.text: With respect to a State that is subject to the requirement under clause (i) and is not in compliance with the requirement for names-based reporting of living non-AIDS cases of HIV, the Secretary shall, notwithstanding such noncompliance, accept reports of living cases of AIDS that are in accordance with such clause.

children.heading: Applicability of exemption requirements
children.label: II Applicability of exemption requirements
children.text: The provisions of clauses (ii) through (vii) may not be construed as having any legal effect for fiscal year 2013 or any subsequent fiscal year, and accordingly, the status of a State for purposes of such clauses may not be considered after fiscal year 2012.

children.heading: Program for detecting inaccurate or fraudulent counting
children.label: ix Program for detecting inaccurate or fraudulent counting
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out a program to monitor the reporting of names-based cases for purposes of this subparagraph and to detect instances of inaccurate reporting, including fraudulent reporting.

children.heading: Future fiscal years
children.label: x Future fiscal years
children.text: For fiscal years beginning with fiscal year 2013, determinations under this paragraph shall be based only on living names-based cases of HIV/AIDS with respect to the State involved.

children.heading: Code-based States; limitation on increase in grant
children.label: E Code-based States; limitation on increase in grant

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For each of the fiscal years 2007 through 2012, if code-based reporting (within the meaning of subparagraph (D)(vi)) applies in a State as of the beginning of the fiscal year involved, then notwithstanding any other provision of this paragraph, the amount of the grant pursuant to paragraph (1) for the State may not for the fiscal year involved exceed by more than 5 percent the amount of the grant pursuant to this paragraph for the State for the preceding fiscal year, except that the limitation under this clause may not result in a grant pursuant to paragraph (1) for a fiscal year that is less than the minimum amount that applies to the State under such paragraph for such fiscal year.

children.heading: Use of amounts involved
children.label: ii Use of amounts involved
children.text: For each of the fiscal years 2007 through 2012, amounts available as a result of the limitation under clause (i) shall be made available by the Secretary as additional amounts for grants pursuant to , subject to subparagraph (H).section 300ff–29a of this title

children.heading: Appropriations for treatment drug program
children.label: F Appropriations for treatment drug program
I
children.text: 100 percent of such amount, less the percentage reserved under clause (ii)(V); and

children.label: II
children.text: the percentage constituted by the ratio of the State distribution factor for the State (as determined under subparagraph (B)) to the sum of the State distribution factors for all States;

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: From amounts made available under subclause (V), the Secretary shall award supplemental grants to States described in subclause (II) to enable such States to purchase and distribute to eligible individuals under pharmaceutical therapeutics described under subsections (c)(2) and (e) of such section.section 300ff–26(b) of this title

children.heading: Eligible States
children.label: II Eligible States
children.text: For purposes of subclause (I), a State shall be an eligible State if the State did not have unobligated funds subject to reallocation under subsection (d) in the previous fiscal year and, in accordance with criteria established by the Secretary, demonstrates a severe need for a grant under this clause. For purposes of determining severe need, the Secretary shall consider eligibility standards, formulary composition, the number of eligible individuals to whom a State is unable to provide therapeutics described in , and an unanticipated increase of eligible individuals with HIV/AIDS.section 300ff–26(a) of this title

children.heading: State requirements
children.label: III State requirements
children.text: The Secretary may not make a grant to a State under this clause unless the State agrees that the State will make available (directly or through donations of public or private entities) non-Federal contributions toward the activities to be carried out under the grant in an amount equal to $1 for each $4 of Federal funds provided in the grant, except that the Secretary may waive this subclause if the State has otherwise fully complied with with respect to the grant year involved. The provisions of this subclause shall apply to States that are not required to comply with such .section 300ff–27(d) of this titlesection 300ff–27(d) of this title

children.heading: Use and coordination
children.label: IV Use and coordination
children.text: Amounts made available under a grant under this clause shall only be used by the State to provide HIV/AIDS-related medications. The State shall coordinate the use of such amounts with the amounts otherwise provided under in order to maximize drug coverage.section 300ff–26(a) of this title

children.heading: Funding
children.label: V Funding
children.text: For the purpose of making grants under this clause, the Secretary shall each fiscal year reserve 5 percent of the amount referred to in clause (i) with respect to .section 300ff–26 of this title

children.heading: Code-based States; limitation on increase in formula grant
children.label: iii Code-based States; limitation on increase in formula grant
children.text: The limitation under subparagraph (E)(i) applies to grants pursuant to clause (i) of this subparagraph to the same extent and in the same manner as such limitation applies to grants pursuant to paragraph (1), except that the reference to minimum grants does not apply for purposes of this clause. Amounts available as a result of the limitation under the preceding sentence shall be made available by the Secretary as additional amounts for grants under clause (ii) of this subparagraph.

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 109–415, title II, § 203(b)(2)120 Stat. 2792Dec. 19, 2006
children.label: G Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 109–415, title II, § 203(b)(2)120 Stat. 2792Dec. 19, 2006

children.heading: General rule
children.label: I General rule
children.text: For fiscal year 2010, the Secretary shall ensure, subject to clauses (ii) through (iv), that the total for a State of the grant pursuant to paragraph (1) and the grant pursuant to subparagraph (F) is not less than 95 percent of such total for the State for fiscal year 2009.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: II Rule of construction
children.text: With respect to the application of subclause (I), the 95 percent requirement under such subclause shall apply with respect to each grant awarded under paragraph (1) and with respect to each grant awarded under subparagraph (F).

children.heading: Fiscal years 2011 and 2012
children.label: ii Fiscal years 2011 and 2012
children.text: For each of the fiscal years 2011 and 2012, the Secretary shall ensure that the total for a State of the grant pursuant to paragraph (1) and the grant pursuant to subparagraph (F) is not less than 100 percent of such total for the State for fiscal year 2010.

children.heading: Fiscal year 2013
children.label: iii Fiscal year 2013
children.text: For fiscal year 2013, the Secretary shall ensure that the total for a State of the grant pursuant to paragraph (1) and the grant pursuant to subparagraph (F) is not less than 92.5 percent of such total for the State for fiscal year 2012.

children.heading: Source of funds for increase
children.label: iv Source of funds for increase

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: From the amount reserved under for a fiscal year, and from amounts available for such section pursuant to subsection (d) of this section, the Secretary shall make available such amounts as may be necessary to comply with clause (i).section 300ff–31b(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Pro rata reduction
children.label: II Pro rata reduction
children.text: If the amounts referred to in subclause (I) for a fiscal year are insufficient to fully comply with clause (i) for the year, the Secretary, in order to provide the additional funds necessary for such compliance, shall reduce on a pro rata basis the amount of each grant pursuant to paragraph (1) for the fiscal year, other than grants for States for which increases under clause (i) apply and other than States described in paragraph (1)(A)(i)(I). A reduction under the preceding sentence may not be made in an amount that would result in the State involved becoming eligible for such an increase.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: v Applicability
children.text: This paragraph may not be construed as having any applicability after fiscal year 2013.

children.heading: Allocation of assistance by States
children.label: b Allocation of assistance by States

children.heading: Allowances
children.label: 1 Allowances
children.text: Prior to allocating assistance under this subsection, a State shall consider the unmet needs of those areas that have not received financial assistance under part A.

children.heading: Planning and evaluations
children.label: 2 Planning and evaluations
children.text: Subject to paragraph (4) and except as provided in paragraph (5), a State may not use more than 10 percent of amounts received under a grant awarded under for planning and evaluation activities.section 300ff–21 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (4), and except as provided in paragraph (5), a State may not use more than 10 percent of amounts received under a grant awarded under for administration.section 300ff–21 of this title

children.heading: Allocations
children.label: B Allocations
children.text: In the case of entities and subcontractors to which a State allocates amounts received by the State under a grant under , the State shall ensure that, of the aggregate amount so allocated, the total of the expenditures by such entities for administrative expenses does not exceed 10 percent (without regard to whether particular entities expend more than 10 percent for such expenses).section 300ff–21 of this title

children.heading: Administrative activities
children.label: C Administrative activities
children.text: For the purposes of subparagraph (A), amounts may be used for administrative activities that include routine grant administration and monitoring activities, including a clinical quality management program under subparagraph (E).

children.label: i
children.text: usual and recognized overhead, including established indirect rates for agencies;

children.label: ii
children.text: management oversight of specific programs funded under this subchapter; and

children.label: iii
children.text: other types of program support such as quality assurance, quality control, and related activities.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: i Requirement
children.text: Each State that receives a grant under shall provide for the establishment of a clinical quality management program to assess the extent to which HIV health services provided to patients under the grant are consistent with the most recent Public Health Service guidelines for the treatment of HIV/AIDS and related opportunistic infection, and as applicable, to develop strategies for ensuring that such services are consistent with the guidelines for improvement in the access to and quality of HIV health services.section 300ff–21 of this title

children.label: aa
children.text: 5 percent of amounts received under the grant; or

children.heading: Relation to limitation on administrative expenses
children.label: II Relation to limitation on administrative expenses
children.text: The costs of a clinical quality management program under clause (i) may not be considered administrative expenses for purposes of the limitation established in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Limitation on use of funds
children.label: 4 Limitation on use of funds
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (5), a State may not use more than a total of 15 percent of amounts received under a grant awarded under for the purposes described in paragraphs (2) and (3).section 300ff–21 of this title

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 5 Exception
children.text: With respect to a State that receives the minimum allotment under subsection (a)(1) for a fiscal year, such State, from the amounts received under a grant awarded under for such fiscal year for the activities described in paragraphs (2) and (3), may, notwithstanding paragraphs (2) through (4), use not more than that amount required to support one full-time-equivalent employee.section 300ff–21 of this title

children.heading: Construction
children.label: 6 Construction
children.text: A State may not use amounts received under a grant awarded under to purchase or improve land, or to purchase, construct, or permanently improve (other than minor remodeling) any building or other facility, or to make cash payments to intended recipients of services.section 300ff–21 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of the first fiscal year for which amounts are received, 150 days after the receipt of such amounts by the State; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of succeeding fiscal years, 120 days after the receipt of such amounts by the State.

children.heading: Public comment
children.label: 2 Public comment
children.text: Within the time periods referred to in paragraph (1), the State shall invite and receive public comment concerning methods for the utilization of such amounts.

children.heading: Reallocation
children.label: d Reallocation
children.text: Any portion of a grant made to a State under for a fiscal year that has not been obligated as described in subsection (c) ceases to be available to the State and shall be made available by the Secretary for grants under , in addition to amounts made available for such grants under .section 300ff–21 of this titlesection 300ff–29a of this titlesection 300ff–31b(b)(2) of this title
